notes: Auto-executed tool call, ungated: the user asks opencode to perform one tool action (write a file with its write tool) and reply done. In headless default (`opencode run`) the tool auto-executes inside opencode's sandbox with NO interactive permission prompt, so the canonical arc is ready→working→ready and state NEVER enters waiting. Distinct from the sibling full-lifecycle-toolcall (same coverage_id) which deliberately surfaces waiting via a gate; this cell locks down the auto-approved happy path. Session state lives in opencode's SQLite store (~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db), observed by the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore source as the UUID-keyed ses_… session. The tool call is observable in the store / transcript.jsonl as opencode function_call / function_call_output events (parser.go parseToolPart: tool part pending/running → function_call, completed → function_call_output); the turn settles working→ready when the parser sees step-finish reason=stop → turn_done. The phase DSL has no tool-event form, so the auto-executed tool call is asserted via the single uninterrupted working span it sustains plus the no-waiting invariant, not a dedicated phase — verified in the SQLite store out-of-band at record time. session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn_start (matching working) establishes the ses_… session identity that turn_done chains off via same_session_as. Same proven shape as the sibling opencode turn-end-terminal-text and token-accounting specs; opencode's driver runs a blocking `opencode run` per turn (not a held-open REPL), so the cell asserts the arc and omits a separate teardown/process_exited phase like its opencode siblings.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch
PID bound to the ses_… session
User prompt requiring a tool call drives the ses_… session ready → working
The tool auto-executes (no approval gate) inside the working span and the turn completes → back to ready on the same ses_… session; state never entered waiting
